response.justification: Candidate is dramatically overqualified for an entry-level role — they have 15+ years of experience as a Senior/Staff Software Engineer, while the position targets fresh graduates with initial project experience. Java is listed but not a core competency (primary stacks are TypeScript, Node.js, DLang). Critically, German is self-rated as 'Basic' while the role requires strong verbal and written German for client work in the insurance sector. Hiring would create misalignment on level, compensation expectations, and language fit.
strengths: Deep, broad engineering experience across web, backend, embedded, and cloud
Already based in Berlin with strong agile/TDD/CI-CD practice
Has some Java exposure (Altom, National Instruments) plus OOP fluency
concerns: Severely overqualified — Staff/Senior level applying for Junior role
German skills only 'Basic'; role requires strong German for insurance clients
Java is peripheral to candidate's stack; primary expertise is TS/Node/DLang

key_factors.factor: Insufficient German for client-facing insurance work
